You shouldn't really put your damage units on the front, unless if you want to intentionally have them dead (certain wheels benefits from these/the deck becomes thinner if an awaker dies=better cards for most turns).

If you're running helot, you should probably commit to the helot team (scroll to Kade's messages to see some example of helot teams, he usually explains how it works, you can adjust it if you don't have the necessary wheels/awakers)

You can also run a proper poison team, although you should put nymph at the back, maybe have 24/Lotan with pandia SSR(candle) or the poison SR(I forgot the name) wheel to steal alienus/apply more poison. You can also use 24 + Erica because Erica can paralyze an awaker for 1 arith, and you can use 24's paralysis poison card for double poison 
(Paralysis mechanic:

First time applied in the CURRENT turn:
Normal damage + disables strike until next turn.

Second time applied on the same target in the CURRENT turn:
Double damage instead as the target is already in the paralysis state

Applied paralysis on the same target NEXT subsequent turns: normal damage + disables strike PLUS transforms the strike of the affected awaker to illusions. You can only do this every time you paralyze every 2nd time you do. (Read the status effect below the awaker for a clear detail.)). Basically, if you read the first and second paragraph, the intended combo is to paralyze with Erica first for the status, then paralyze with 24 for double damage (which also means double poison).
I can provide a few poison/helot teams that may work with what you have if you'd like.

Also unrelated to any of this question and need of help... In my language Castor's name would translate to "beaver"

Tawil provides 1 barrier to everyone.
Analysis of death wheel gives 1 barrier to remaining allies when the holder dies.
Salv's, Tawil's, and Corposant's SSR wheels grants 1 barrier to the holder every turn.
Doresein gives 2 barrier.
Castor SR gives a 1 cost 1 barrier to the lowest HP Ally every turn.
Helping hand gives 3 barrier to the lowest HP Ally upon exalt.
Sorel posse gives 2 barrier.
Corposant posse gives 1 barrier to everyone.
Ghelot posse gives 3 barrier.
Marvelous buffs can give barrier if you believe hard enough.

Reinforce doesn't prevent direct attacks, instead halves any direct status infliction or damage dealt. (Status stacks still does damage indirectly, so while 40 bleed becomes 20 bleed if applied through, it will still deal 20 regardless. 40 bleeding before reinforce will still do 40 damage aswell even if the reinforce was applied afterwards.)

Another "buff" that "prevents damage" (delays damage till next turn) is Arachne's exalt
